abstract.t: The Lowest Common Denominator Protocol (LCDP) is a message-oriented, peer-to-peer wire format consisting of UTF-8 encoded JSON arrays of externally tagged objects transported over datagrams. LCDP provides perpetual compatibility by extension rather than versioning: unknown message types and fields are ignored. This document describes the wire format, core message types for peer discovery and anti- spoofing, and the design rationale. Security, reliability, and congestion control are delegated to optional messages or higher layers.
